title: Task 1: commit tool — typed, validating, executing against a temp git repo
description:
## Goal

Deliver a working, test-driven `commit` tool end-to-end: typed schema registered with Pi, validation for subject and file paths, real git staging + commit via `pi.exec` against a temp repo, structured success and failure shapes. This is the load-bearing primitive; the guard, slash command, and SKILL.md are built on top in later tasks.

**Execution mode:** HITL. Strict TDD is new, the test infrastructure for a temp-git-repo integration is new, and the user explicitly wants every behavior walked through a full RED-GREEN-REFACTOR cycle with human confirmation between cycles.

## Behaviors

Each behavior is exactly one RED-GREEN-REFACTOR cycle. Run `cape:test-driven-development` per behavior. Do not batch. Do not pre-write implementation ahead of its test. For each cycle: write the failing test, run it and prove it fails with the expected message, write the minimum code to pass, run it and prove it passes, consider refactor (explicitly choose refactor or "no refactor needed"), then move to the next behavior. No single commit should contain more than one behavior's RED → GREEN transition.

1. **`validateSubject` throws a validation error naming the subject when the input does not match the conventional-commit regex.** Drives: `tool.ts` file creation, `types.ts` with `CommitInput` type, minimal `validateSubject(subject: string)` helper throwing unconditionally in GREEN. Test a subject like `"Add stuff."`.

2. **`validateSubject` returns without throwing when the subject matches the conventional-commit regex.** Drives: the actual regex `^(feat|fix|chore|refactor|docs|test|style|perf|build|ci|revert)(\([a-z0-9-]+\))?!?: .+` extracted as a locked constant in `tool.ts`. Pair with behavior 1 to avoid a tautology (rejects-all). Test `"feat: add thing"`, `"fix(scope): do it"`, `"chore!: breaking"`.

3. **`validatePaths` throws an error naming the offending path when any file matches the sensitive-path denylist.** Drives: `validatePaths(files: string[])` helper, locked denylist constant covering `.env`, `.env.*`, `*credentials*`, `*secret*`, `*.pem`, `*.key`, `id_rsa`, `id_rsa.*`, `id_ed25519`, `id_ed25519.*`, `.ssh/*`. Test with `[".env"]`, `["db/credentials.json"]`, `["keys/id_rsa"]`, `[".ssh/config"]`. The thrown error names which path matched.

4. **`validatePaths` returns without throwing when all files are outside the denylist.** Drives: glob matching logic (not just `includes`). Test with `["src/foo.ts", "README.md", "docs/env.md"]` — note `docs/env.md` must NOT match because it is not `.env` nor `.env.*`. Pair with behavior 3 to avoid a rejects-all tautology.

5. **The `commit` tool creates exactly one commit on a temp git repo when given valid inputs, and `details.sha` matches `git rev-parse HEAD`.** Drives: the full `execute()` path — `pi.exec("git", ["add", ...files])`, `pi.exec("git", ["commit", "-m", subject])`, capture of stdout for sha parsing, return of the success shape `{ content: [{ type: "text", text: "<sha> <subject>" }], details: { sha, files, subject } }`. Drives: `index.ts` with `pi.registerTool(commitTool)`. Test: create a temp directory, `git init`, write one tracked file, call the tool, assert `git log --oneline | wc -l` is 1, the subject appears in `git log -1`, and the returned sha matches `git rev-parse HEAD`.

6. **When `body` is provided, the committed message contains `<subject>\n\n<body>`.** Drives: message assembly logic in `execute()`. Test: call the tool with `subject: "feat: add"`, `body: "Longer explanation here."`; assert `git log -1 --format=%B` on the temp repo matches exactly `feat: add\n\nLonger explanation here.\n`.

7. **When `git commit` exits non-zero (pre-commit hook fails), the tool throws an error whose detail contains `hookFailed: true`, `stderr`, and `stdout`.** Drives: `HookFailedError` class (or equivalent structured throw), exit-code inspection after `pi.exec`. Test: create a temp repo whose `.git/hooks/pre-commit` is a script that writes to stderr and exits 1, stage a file, call the tool, assert the thrown error has `error.detail?.hookFailed === true`, `error.detail.stderr` non-empty, `error.detail.stdout` defined, AND assert `git log` on the repo still shows zero commits (the failed attempt did not create one).

## References

Similar patterns to look at while implementing:

- `node_modules/@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent/docs/extensions.md:1030-1092` — `pi.registerTool` with typebox schema, `execute()` signature, `pi.exec` usage, error semantics (throw to set `isError`).
- `node_modules/@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent/examples/extensions/auto-commit-on-exit.ts:11-49` — real-world example of `pi.exec("git", [...])` for staging and committing, including exit-code inspection.
- `node_modules/@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent/dist/core/extensions/loader.js:189` — confirms `pi.exec` is a direct `execCommand` delegation (bypasses `tool_call` event).
- `docs/adr/0002-file-naming-conventions.md` — camelCase `.ts`, colocated `*.test.ts`, no barrel `index.ts`.
- `docs/adr/0003-externally-observable-identifiers.md` — wire name `commit` and the success/failure return shapes are contracts; do not rename as a side effect of a refactor.
- `docs/adr/0004-skill-authoring-style.md` — relevant later; this task writes no SKILL.md.
- User rules `testing-typescript.md` and `testing-go.md` — prefer real infrastructure; tests use a real temp git repo, never mock `pi.exec`.
- `vitest` is the test runner (`package.json:22`); `pnpm test` runs it.

## Success criteria

- [ ] `src/extensions/commit/tool.ts` exists with the `commit` tool definition and typebox schema `{ files: Array<string>, subject: string, body?: string }`.
- [ ] `src/extensions/commit/types.ts` exists with `CommitInput`, `CommitSuccess`, `CommitHookFailure` types. No value code.
- [ ] `src/extensions/commit/index.ts` exists, imports from `tool.ts`, exports a default Pi wiring function that calls `pi.registerTool(commitTool)`. No `pi.registerCommand` or `pi.on` calls in this task (those are later tasks).
- [ ] `src/extensions/index.ts` imports and installs the commit extension.
- [ ] `src/extensions/commit/tool.test.ts` exists with one test per behavior above, plus shared temp-repo setup/teardown helpers.
- [ ] Every test uses a real temp git repository; `pi.exec` is never mocked; `validateSubject` / `validatePaths` are exercised through their real call sites (the tool's `execute`) where the behavior is end-to-end, or directly as unit tests for pure validation behaviors.
- [ ] After the task is complete, `pi.getAllTools()` (or equivalent access after extension load) contains an entry with `name: "commit"`.
- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` passes.
- [ ] `pnpm lint` passes.
- [ ] `pnpm lint:rules` passes.
- [ ] `pnpm format:check` passes.
- [ ] `pnpm test` passes with all new tests green.
- [ ] Git history for this task shows one RED-GREEN(-REFACTOR) cycle per behavior. Evidence: each behavior has at least one commit whose diff contains a failing test (RED) followed by a commit (or same commit with demonstrated prior failure) containing the implementation (GREEN). No behavior's implementation lands before its test.
- [ ] No file outside `src/extensions/commit/`, `src/extensions/index.ts`, and `src/extensions/commit/tool.test.ts` is modified except `package.json` (only if a new devDep is genuinely required for this task, which it should not be — `@sinclair/typebox` and `vitest` should already be available transitively).
- [ ] `src/extensions/commit/tool.ts` source contains no tokens for bulk-add, skip-hooks, or rewrite-last-commit variants.
- [ ] No `skills/commit/SKILL.md`, `src/extensions/commit/guard.ts`, or `src/extensions/commit/shell/` exist after this task (those are later tasks; this slice intentionally stops at the tool).
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks pass on the final commit of the task.

The GREEN change introduced a conventional-commit regex constant covering feat|fix|chore|refactor|docs|test|style|perf|build|ci|revert, optional lowercase scope, optional bang, colon-space, and trailing text; validateSubject now returns for matching subjects and throws only for non-matches. GREEN: added locked `sensitivePathDenylist` regex constant and minimal `validatePaths(files)` helper that throws `Invalid path: <file>` on the first match. The current denylist covers `.env`, `.env.*`, credentials, secret, `.pem`, `.key`, id_rsa, id_ed25519, and `.ssh/*`. `pnpm test -- src/extensions/commit/tool.test.ts` now passes. That means behavior 4 was already satisfied by the behavior-3 implementation rather than producing a fresh RED step. The important semantic check is now locked in by test: `docs/env.md` does not match the denylist.

comments.author: sqve
comments.text:
Behaviors 5-7 are now implemented and green. Behavior 5: added a real temp-repo integration test that loads `src/extensions/index.ts` into a fake Pi API, verifies the `commit` tool is registered by name, executes the tool against a temp git repo via `pi.exec`, and asserts exactly one commit exists, the subject is present in `git log -1 --format=%s`, and `details.sha` matches `git rev-parse HEAD`. Behavior 6: added body support and verified the stored commit message is exactly `feat: add

Longer explanation here.
`. I had to verify this with `git cat-file -p HEAD` instead of `git log -1 --format=%B` because in this environment `%B` appends an extra separator newline (`

`), which appears to make the issue texts exact command/output claim misleading. Behavior 7: added a temp-repo pre-commit hook that writes to stdout/stderr and exits 1; the tool now throws `HookFailedError` with `detail = { hookFailed: true, stdout, stderr }`, and the test asserts the repo still has zero commits after failure.

title: Task 2: shell parser — tree-sitter-bash wasm, parseBash, findGitCommits
description:
## Goal

Deliver an extension-local shell parser at `src/extensions/commit/shell/` that loads `tree-sitter-bash` via `web-tree-sitter`, parses bash command strings into ASTs, and walks them to detect git-commit invocations. This is the detection engine the guard (Task 3) will consume.

**Execution mode:** HITL. First use of tree-sitter in the project, wasm loading is new, and strict TDD applies.

## Behaviors

Each behavior is one RED-GREEN-REFACTOR cycle.

1. **`parseBash` returns a tree-sitter AST for a simple command string.** Drives: `shell/index.ts` creation, lazy wasm parser singleton, `parseBash(command: string)` function. Drives: `vendor/tree-sitter-bash.wasm` committed, `web-tree-sitter` added to devDependencies. Test: parse `"echo hello"`, assert root node type is `"program"`, child count > 0.

2. **`findGitCommits` returns an empty array for non-commit git commands.** Drives: `shell/types.ts` with `GitCommitHit` type, `findGitCommits` function. Test with negative corpus: `"git status"`, `"git diff HEAD"`, `"git log --oneline"`, `"git add src/foo.ts"`, `"echo 'git commit -m foo'"` (literal string mention). All return `[]`.

3. **`findGitCommits` detects a plain `git commit` invocation.** Test: `"git commit -m 'feat: add'"` returns one hit. Assert hit includes the node range or command text.

4. **`findGitCommits` detects `git commit` with the rewrite-last-commit flag variant.** Test: `"git commit --amend -m 'fix: typo'"` returns one hit with an `amend: true` flag (or equivalent marker distinguishing it from plain commit).

5. **`findGitCommits` detects `git commit` inside `&&`-chains, pipelines, command substitutions, and subshells.** Test corpus: `"git add . && git commit -m 'feat: x'"`, `"echo foo | git commit --allow-empty-message"`, `"echo $(git commit -m 'x')"`, `"(git commit -m 'x')"`. Each returns at least one hit.

6. **`findGitCommits` detects hyphen-dispatched `git-commit`.** Test: `"git-commit -m 'feat: add'"` returns one hit.

7. **`findGitCommits` returns an empty array for pipe-to-grep patterns that mention commit as a string argument.** Test: `"git log | grep commit"`, `"git log --format='%s' | grep 'commit'"`. Both return `[]`. This pairs with behavior 3 to prevent over-matching.

## References

- `node_modules/@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent/docs/extensions.md` — npm dependency support under jiti.
- [tree-sitter/tree-sitter-bash](https://github.com/tree-sitter/tree-sitter-bash) — official grammar, wasm target.
- `web-tree-sitter` npm package — wasm consumer for tree-sitter grammars.
- `~/.claude/projects/-home-sqve-code-personal-tau/memory/feedback_shell_parsing.md` — user feedback: prefer tree-sitter over regex/handwritten parsers.
- `docs/adr/0001-application-structure.md` — `vendor/` provision for third-party binary assets.
- `docs/adr/0002-file-naming-conventions.md` — camelCase `.ts`, colocated tests.

## Success criteria

- [ ] `src/extensions/commit/shell/index.ts` exists with `parseBash` and `findGitCommits` exports.
- [ ] `src/extensions/commit/shell/types.ts` exists with `GitCommitHit` type. No value code.
- [ ] `src/extensions/commit/shell/index.test.ts` exists with one test per behavior.
- [ ] `vendor/tree-sitter-bash.wasm` committed with an adjacent version note file.
- [ ] `package.json` declares `web-tree-sitter` in devDependencies. `pnpm install` succeeds.
- [ ] Parser loads lazily from a shared singleton promise (not re-initialized per call).
- [ ] `findGitCommits` walks `command_name` nodes, recurses into `command_substitution` and `subshell` children.
- [ ] Positive corpus (behaviors 3-6) all detected. Negative corpus (behaviors 2, 7) all clean.
- [ ] No regex-based command matching — all detection via tree-sitter AST walking.
- [ ] Tests use real wasm parser, never mocked.
- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` passes.
- [ ] `pnpm lint` passes.
- [ ] `pnpm lint:rules` passes.
- [ ] `pnpm format:check` passes.
- [ ] `pnpm test` passes with all new tests green.
- [ ] No files outside `src/extensions/commit/shell/`, `vendor/`, and `package.json`/`pnpm-lock.yaml` modified.
- [ ] No `guard.ts` or `guard.test.ts` in this task (that is Task 3).
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks pass on the final commit.

title: Task 3: guard — tool_call handler blocking LLM-initiated bash git-commit
description:
## Goal

Deliver a `tool_call` event handler at `src/extensions/commit/guard.ts` that intercepts LLM-initiated bash calls, delegates to the shell parser from Task 2, and blocks git-commit invocations with a reason referencing the `commit` tool. Also includes the literal-substring secondary check for `sh -c` / `bash -c` eval bypasses. Wire the guard into `src/extensions/commit/index.ts` via `pi.on("tool_call", ...)`.

**Execution mode:** HITL. Guard correctness is critical — false positives break the workflow, false negatives defeat the purpose.

## Behaviors

Each behavior is one RED-GREEN-REFACTOR cycle.

1. **Guard returns undefined (no block) for non-bash tool calls.** Drives: `guard.ts` creation, handler function narrowing with `isToolCallEventType("bash", event)`. Test: pass a tool_call event with `toolName: "read"` — handler returns undefined.

2. **Guard returns undefined for bash commands that are not git-commit invocations.** Test with negative corpus from Task 2: `"git status"`, `"git diff HEAD"`, `"git log --oneline"`, `"git add src/foo.ts"`. All return undefined.

3. **Guard returns `{ block: true, reason }` for a plain `git commit` bash command.** Test: event with `input.command: "git commit -m 'feat: add'"`. Assert `block: true` and reason string references the `commit` tool.

4. **Guard blocks the full positive corpus.** Test: `"git commit --amend"`, `"git add . && git commit -m 'x'"`, `"echo $(git commit -m 'x')"`, `"(git commit -m 'x')"`, `"git-commit -m 'feat: add'"`. All return `{ block: true }`.

5. **Guard blocks `sh -c` and `bash -c` containing commit invocations via literal-substring check.** Test: `"sh -c 'git commit -m x'"`, `"bash -c 'git commit -m x'"`. Both return `{ block: true }`.

6. **Guard does NOT block `sh -c` or `bash -c` without commit invocations.** Test: `"sh -c 'git status'"`, `"bash -c 'echo hello'"`. Both return undefined.

7. **Guard does NOT block pipe-to-grep patterns mentioning commit.** Test: `"git log | grep commit"`. Returns undefined.

8. **Guard is registered in `index.ts` and does not fire on the tool's own git invocations.** Drives: update `src/extensions/commit/index.ts` to call `pi.on("tool_call", guardHandler)`. Test: end-to-end — run the commit tool against a temp repo (reuse Task 1 infrastructure), verify no spurious block events.

## References

- `src/extensions/commit/shell/index.ts` — `parseBash`, `findGitCommits` from Task 2.
- `node_modules/@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent/docs/extensions.md` — `pi.on("tool_call", ...)`, `isToolCallEventType`.
- `dist/core/agent-session.js:166-186` — `beforeToolCall` fires only for LLM-dispatched tool calls.
- Epic design: literal-substring secondary check is a fixed rule, not a parser extension.

## Success criteria

- [ ] `src/extensions/commit/guard.ts` exists with the guard handler export.
- [ ] `src/extensions/commit/guard.test.ts` exists with one test per behavior.
- [ ] `src/extensions/commit/index.ts` updated to register guard via `pi.on("tool_call", guardHandler)`.
- [ ] Guard blocks positive corpus: plain commit, rewrite-last-commit variant, `&&`-chained, piped, command-substitution, subshell, hyphen-dispatched, `sh -c`/`bash -c` eval variants.
- [ ] Guard does NOT block negative corpus: status, diff, log, `git add <path>`, literal-string mentions, pipe-to-grep.
- [ ] Guard does NOT fire on the tool's own git invocations (end-to-end test).
- [ ] Reason message references the `commit` tool by wire name.
- [ ] No regex-based command detection in the guard — all AST-based via shell parser (except the literal-substring `sh -c`/`bash -c` secondary check).
- [ ] Tests use real wasm parser, never mocked.
- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` passes.
- [ ] `pnpm lint` passes.
- [ ] `pnpm lint:rules` passes.
- [ ] `pnpm format:check` passes.
- [ ] `pnpm test` passes with all tests green.
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks pass on the final commit.

title: Task 4: /commit slash command — thin entrypoint delegating to skill
description:
## Goal

Register a `/commit` slash command via `pi.registerCommand` that delegates to the commit skill via `pi.sendUserMessage`. The handler picks `deliverAs: "followUp"` when idle, `deliverAs: "steer"` while streaming. This is a thin entrypoint — no feature logic, no argument parsing beyond forwarding.

**Execution mode:** HITL. First slash command in tau; need to verify Pi's command registration and `sendUserMessage` behavior.

## Behaviors

Each behavior is one RED-GREEN-REFACTOR cycle.

1. **`/commit` is registered as an extension command with `name: "commit"` and `source: "extension"`.** Drives: update `src/extensions/commit/index.ts` to call `pi.registerCommand("commit", { ... })`. Test: load the extension, verify `pi.getCommands()` contains an entry with `name: "commit"`.

2. **Handler calls `pi.sendUserMessage` with `deliverAs: "followUp"` when idle.** Test: simulate `ctx.isIdle() === true`, verify `sendUserMessage` was called with the skill prefix and `{ deliverAs: "followUp" }`.

3. **Handler calls `pi.sendUserMessage` with `deliverAs: "steer"` while streaming.** Test: simulate `ctx.isIdle() === false`, verify `sendUserMessage` was called with `{ deliverAs: "steer" }`.

4. **Handler forwards trailing arguments to the skill message.** Test: invoke with args `"--scope auth"`, verify `sendUserMessage` receives `"/skill:commit --scope auth"` (or equivalent concatenation).

5. **Handler sends `/skill:commit` with no trailing space when invoked with no arguments.** Test: invoke with empty args, verify message is exactly `"/skill:commit"`.

## References

- `node_modules/@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent/docs/extensions.md` — `pi.registerCommand`, `pi.sendUserMessage`, `ctx.isIdle()`, `deliverAs` options.
- Epic design: command is primary entrypoint; skill expansion fires on re-entry via `/skill:commit` prefix.
- `dist/core/agent-session.js:812-814` — `_expandSkillCommand` only fires on `/skill:` prefix.

## Success criteria

- [ ] `src/extensions/commit/index.ts` calls `pi.registerCommand("commit", { ... })`.
- [ ] `/commit` appears in `pi.getCommands()` with `name: "commit"`, `source: "extension"`.
- [ ] Idle invocation triggers `sendUserMessage` with `deliverAs: "followUp"`.
- [ ] Streaming invocation triggers `sendUserMessage` with `deliverAs: "steer"`.
- [ ] Arguments forwarded; no-arg invocation sends bare `/skill:commit`.
- [ ] No feature logic in the handler — delegation only.
- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` passes.
- [ ] `pnpm lint` passes.
- [ ] `pnpm lint:rules` passes.
- [ ] `pnpm format:check` passes.
- [ ] `pnpm test` passes with all tests green.
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks pass on the final commit.
close_reason: Implemented /commit slash command delegating to /skill:commit and validated with tests

title: Task 5: SKILL.md — commit skill procedure per ADR-0004
description:
## Goal

Write `skills/commit/SKILL.md` — the Markdown-first skill body that drives the LLM through the commit workflow. Conforms to ADR-0004: frontmatter with \`name: commit\`, body sections from the allowed set, no XML-like tags. The skill hard-depends on the \`commit\` tool with no fallback.

**Execution mode:** Standard. This is prose, not code — no TDD cycles, but the file must pass structural validation.

## Behaviors

1. **Frontmatter contains \`name: commit\` and a specific description.** The description explains what the skill does in one sentence.

2. **Body uses only ADR-0004 section names.** Allowed sections: \`When to use\`, \`Goal\`, \`Hard rules\`, \`Procedure\`, \`Checklist\`, \`See also\`. No XML-tagged sections.

3. **Procedure instructs the LLM to:** gather git state (\`git status --porcelain\`, \`git diff\`), identify logical groups of changes, propose each group with a subject and file list for user confirmation, call the \`commit\` tool per confirmed group, handle \`hookFailed\` errors by reading stderr and fixing the underlying issue, cap retries at 3 per group, loop until the working tree is clean or the user stops.

4. **Hard rules section includes:** use the \`commit\` tool (no bash \`git commit\`), never stage with \`-A\` or \`.\`, never pass \`--no-verify\`, never rewrite history (\`--amend\`), always get user confirmation before each commit, conventional-commit subject format.

5. **Skill references the \`commit\` tool by its exact wire name.** No fallback path documented. Missing tool = loud failure.

6. **Hook failure handling:** on \`hookFailed\`, read the \`stderr\` field, diagnose the failure, fix the issue (lint, format, test), re-stage, retry. Cap at 3 retries per group. After 3 failures, stop and report to the user.

## References

- \`docs/adr/0004-skill-authoring-style.md\` — section names, frontmatter shape, no XML tags.
- Epic design: retry cap at 3, evidence-driven hook failure handling, no auto-fix in the tool.
- \`package.json:29-31\` — \`pi.skills = ["./skills"]\` declares the skill directory.

## Success criteria

- [ ] \`skills/commit/SKILL.md\` exists.
- [ ] Frontmatter has \`name: commit\` and a \`description\` field.
- [ ] Body sections are drawn exclusively from ADR-0004 allowed names.
- [ ] No XML-like section tags (\`<section>\`, \`</section>\`, etc.).
- [ ] Procedure covers: git state gathering, logical grouping, user confirmation, tool invocation, hook-failure retry (capped at 3), loop-until-clean.
- [ ] Hard rules cover: use \`commit\` tool only, no \`-A\`/\`.\`, no \`--no-verify\`, no \`--amend\`, user confirmation required, conventional-commit format.
- [ ] Tool referenced by wire name \`commit\` — no fallback path.
- [ ] \`pnpm typecheck\` passes (no TS changes expected, but verify).
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks pass on the final commit.
close_reason: Implemented skills/commit/SKILL.md per ADR-0004 and verified pnpm typecheck.

title: Task 6: correct web-tree-sitter criterion and close epic
description:
## Goal

Housekeeping: fix the one incorrect success criterion and close the epic.

## Behaviors

1. Update the epic success criterion from "package.json declares web-tree-sitter in devDependencies" to "package.json declares web-tree-sitter in dependencies" — it is a runtime dependency loaded by the extension via jiti.
2. Close the epic.

## Success criteria

- [ ] Epic criterion updated to say `dependencies`.
- [ ] Epic closed.

title: build Tau commit flow for Pi
description: Define and implement Tau's opinionated commit flow for Pi. The flow should enforce conventional commits, guide users toward atomic/selectively staged commits, and ensure Pi routes commit requests through Tau-owned UX instead of ad hoc git commands.
acceptance_criteria:
- co-resident unit tests cover all pure commit-flow domain logic introduced by this feature
- end-to-end tests cover the primary happy path and the key blocked-path enforcement behaviors
- no child task is considered complete without explicit verification evidence (tests, manual flow proof, or both)
- Tau routes commit requests through Tau-owned flow instead of ad hoc git commit usage
close_reason: End-to-end commit-flow coverage added; acceptance criteria now satisfied

- child tasks include verification evidence: satisfied; each closed task included tests, formatting checks, or manual flow proof

title: write Tau commit policy and message guidance
description: Capture the commit philosophy discussed in this session as a durable policy: one logical change per commit, selective staging, clear conventional-commit subjects, optional bodies that explain why, inverted-pyramid ordering, and examples/anti-patterns. This becomes the reference Tau and contributors follow.

title: design Tau extension architecture for commit flow
description: Design the extension-first architecture that makes Pi use Tau's commit flow. Cover the /commit command, a tau_commit tool for model-driven commits, prompt injection, bash interception for unsafe git patterns, approval UX, and how the extension coordinates staging and commit execution.
acceptance_criteria:
- architecture documents the planned /commit command, tau_commit tool, prompt injection, bash interception, approval UX, and staging/commit coordination boundaries
- co-resident unit tests cover all pure state and architecture-supporting logic added in this task
- the implemented architecture is validated with passing tests and typechecking
- any follow-on behavior that cannot yet be exercised end to end is explicitly called out for later E2E coverage
close_reason: Done: commit-flow architecture ADR accepted; extension module implemented and tested

title: define conventional commit enforcement and bypass rules
description: Specify the enforceable commit rules Tau owns. Include the allowed conventional-commit types, subject-line requirements, confirmation rules, blocking of git add . / git add -A / raw git commit / --no-verify unless explicitly requested, and the boundary between hard enforcement and softer writing guidance.
acceptance_criteria:
- co-resident unit tests cover conventional-commit validation, bypass classification, and command-blocking rules
- end-to-end tests cover at least: valid commit flow, invalid commit message rejection, raw git commit blocking, and unsafe staging command blocking
- enforcement clearly separates hard blocking rules from softer guidance
- allowed bypasses and their confirmation requirements are documented and verified
close_reason: Done: added conventional-commit validation, bypass classification, command guards, docs, and tests

title: draft companion commit skill for philosophy and examples
description: Create the companion Pi skill that teaches the commit flow rather than enforcing it. It should include the commit philosophy, examples of good and bad commit messages, heuristics for splitting logical changes, and guidance on when a body is warranted.
close_reason: Implemented companion commit skill with verification evidence

title: align repo-level hooks and CI with Tau commit policy

title: add end-to-end integration coverage for Tau commit flow
description:
Add integration and end-to-end tests that exercise Tau commit flow across command guards, repo hook validation, and commit-range validation. Cover the primary happy path, key blocked paths, and important bypass/corner cases so the feature is proven as a whole rather than only through unit tests.

Acceptance Criteria:
- tests cover the primary happy path for a valid commit flow
- tests cover blocked raw `git commit` without explicit override
- tests cover blocked blanket staging without explicit override
- tests cover blocked `--no-verify` without explicit override
- tests cover repo `commit-msg` hook behavior against real message files
- tests cover commit-range validation against temporary git history
- tests cover key bypass-shaped messages and at least one important corner case
- verification evidence includes test execution output
close_reason: Added integration/E2E commit-flow coverage with passing verification
